items.title: When to apply a scaler
items.mermaid:
flowchart TD
  startNode["numeric features ready"] --> algo{"model family?"}
  algo -->|"k-NN, k-means, SVM-RBF"| scaleDist["StandardScaler or MinMax"]
  algo -->|"Ridge, Lasso, neural net"| scaleGrad["StandardScaler typical"]
  algo -->|"tree / boosting"| skipScale["scaling usually optional"]
  scaleDist --> pipe["fit scaler on train only"]
  scaleGrad --> pipe
  pipe --> transform["transform val / test / prod"]
  skipScale --> model["train estimator"]
  transform --> model
items.caption: Match the scaler to algorithms that use distance or shared gradient/penalty scales; trees rarely need rescaling for correctness.

items.title: Train-only fit prevents leakage
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  split["train / val / test split"] --> fit["scaler.fit on X_train"]
  fit --> tTrain["transform X_train"]
  fit --> tVal["transform X_val"]
  fit --> tTest["transform X_test"]
  tTrain --> est["fit estimator"]
  tVal --> eval["evaluate"]
  tTest --> eval
items.caption: Statistics (mean, std, min, max) come from training data only; the same parameters transform all splits.
